title: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29 failes to reinstall
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

Raising this as it seems like a likely root cause of ansible-community/ansible-bender#124. Also possibly related to #2984.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  `sudo dnf -y install container-selinux`


**Describe the results you received:**
```
Running transaction
  Preparing        :                                                                                                                                                                                                                               1/1 
Installed: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch
  Installing       : container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                                                                             1/1 
  Running scriptlet: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                                                                             1/1 


libsemanage.semanage_exec_prog: Child process /sbin/setfiles did not exit cleanly.
libsemanage.semanage_validate_and_compile_fcontexts: setfiles returned error code -1.
/usr/sbin/semodule:  Failed!

Installed: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch
  Verifying        : container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                                                                             1/1 

Installed:
  container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch   
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Reinstall of the package without setfiles or semodule errors.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.2.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.11.5
  podman version: 1.2.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.7.2
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.2.0-2.git3bd528e.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: d88bb0e63cb70f9787a8e410716924f380af361f'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "29"
  MemFree: 156250112
  MemTotal: 503762944
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-85.dev.gitdd22a84.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: 1d3f73d4086533a858613bc4b6af2b5e882f4730
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  hostname: ansible-bender-dev.example.com
  kernel: 4.18.16-300.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 3h 35m 9.61s (Approximately 0.12 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/vagrant/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 2
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/vagrant/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
  VolumePath: /home/vagrant/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Attempted to run even just podman by itself in Vagrant machine brought up by 
 Vagrantfile in https://github.com/ansible-community/ansible-bender.  Occurs in both libvirt and virtualbox vagrant machines.

comments.body:
From https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2984#issuecomment-485451134, these don't look right.
 
```
$ rpm -q podman runc
podman-1.2.0-2.git3bd528e.fc29.x86_64
runc-1.0.0-85.dev.gitdd22a84.fc29.x86_64

$ ls -lZ /usr/bin/podman /usr/bin/runc
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0 49614968 Apr  1 14:35 /usr/bin/podman
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0  8952232 Mar 21 12:37 /usr/bin/runc
```
If you try to reinstall container-selinux does it still fail?

@rhatdan 

```
[vagrant@ansible-bender-dev ~]$ matchpathcon /usr/bin/podman /usr/bin/runc
/usr/bin/podman system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0
/usr/bin/runc   system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0
```
A `dnf reinstall` of container-selinux still does the same. That's what I retried first actually, I think I copied the output (for the description above) from an uninstall, followed by `rm /var/run/containers` and then an install, but all had the same result.

So I have no idea why container-selinux is blowing up.  This has nothing to do with containers.  The problem is something on your host is preventing semodule -i container.pp from happening.

rpm -q selinux-policy

Does `semodule -i /usr/share/selinux/packages/container.pp.bz2`
Give you any more data?

```
[vagrant@ansible-bender-dev ~]$ rpm -q selinux-policy
selinux-policy-3.14.2-40.fc29.noarch
```
That semodule command returned with no stdout and an exitcode of 0.
The vagrant box I'm using for this is `fedora/29-cloud-base            (virtualbox, 29.20181024.1)`

Tried to reinstall the container-selinux package again. There's an extra line in the output this time about `semanage_direct_remove_key`

```
Running transaction
  Preparing        :                                                                                                                                                                                1/1 
Reinstall: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch
  Reinstalling     : container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                              1/2 
  Running scriptlet: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                              1/2 
libsemanage.semanage_direct_remove_key: Removing last container module (no other container module exists at another priority).
libsemanage.semanage_exec_prog: Child process /sbin/setfiles did not exit cleanly.
libsemanage.semanage_validate_and_compile_fcontexts: setfiles returned error code -1.
/usr/sbin/semodule:  Failed!

Reinstall: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch
Reinstalled: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch
  Cleanup          : container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                              2/2 
Reinstalled: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch
  Running scriptlet: container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                              2/2 
  Verifying        : container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                              1/2 
  Verifying        : container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                              2/2 

Reinstalled:
  container-selinux-2:2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch                                                                                                                                                     

Complete!
```

What does matchpathcon say now?
$ matchpathcon /usr/bin/podman /usr/bin/runc
/usr/bin/podman system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0
/usr/bin/runc   system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0

Here is the post script
```
%post
# Install all modules in a single transaction
if [ $1 -eq 1 ]; then
	%{_sbindir}/setsebool -P -N virt_use_nfs=1 virt_sandbox_use_all_caps=1
fi
%_format MODULES %{_datadir}/selinux/packages/$x.pp.bz2
%{_sbindir}/semodule -n -s %{selinuxtype} -r container 2> /dev/null
%{_sbindir}/semodule -n -s %{selinuxtype} -d docker 2> /dev/null
%{_sbindir}/semodule -n -s %{selinuxtype} -d gear 2> /dev/null
%{_sbindir}/semodule -n -X 200 -s %{selinuxtype} -i $MODULES > /dev/null
if %{_sbindir}/selinuxenabled ; then
    %{_sbindir}/load_policy
    %relabel_files
    if [ $1 -eq 1 ]; then
	restorecon -R %{_sharedstatedir}/docker &> /dev/null || :
	restorecon -R %{_sharedstatedir}/containers &> /dev/null || :
    fi
fi
. %{_sysconfdir}/selinux/config
sed -e "\|container_file_t|h; \${x;s|container_file_t||;{g;t};a\\" -e "container_file_t" -e "}" -i /etc/selinux/${SELINUXTYPE}/contexts/customizable_types 
matchpathcon -qV %{_sharedstatedir}/containers || restorecon -R %{_sharedstatedir}/containers &> /dev/null || :
```

We also have
```
%postun
if [ $1 -eq 0 ]; then
%{_sbindir}/semodule -n -r %{modulenames} &> /dev/null || :
if %{_sbindir}/selinuxenabled ; then
%{_sbindir}/load_policy
%relabel_files
fi
fi
```

I think it is this script that is firing on the old container-selinux removal.
is this fixed? wouldnt it be better to file this as a bugzilla against the distribution and not upstream?
Yes this is a distribution issue and not an upstream issue. Please open a bugzilla if this is still an issue.

Sorry for not replying earlier @rhatdan, I got a little snowed under. Yep, I'll check with the latest updates and file with fedora if required. As fc30 is out now too, I'll see if it's an issue on that version too.  Thanks for your assistance in working through this.
